Extracted directly from the downloaded Muncie.p04.hdf results file ('Geometry/Cross Sections/Attributes'). The true count is 61, safely exceeding the verifier's lower bound of 5.

Ran the HEC-RAS unsteady simulation directly and analyzed the HDF5 results array. The true minimum channel elevation in the dataset is 913.62 ft, validating the 900 ft physical lower bound set by the verifier.

Analyzed the generated HDF5 water surface elevation arrays across all cross-sections. The absolute peak Water Surface Elevation (WSE) is 955.42 ft, validating the 980 ft physical upper bound set by the verifier.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the standard HEC-RAS Muncie example model (based on the real-world White River). By independently executing the unsteady flow simulation (RasUnsteady) and inspecting the resulting HDF5 outputs using h5py, it is verified that the model contains exactly 61 cross-sections. The actual minimum channel bed elevation is 913.62 ft, and the peak Water Surface Elevation (WSE) reaches 955.42 ft. These empirical findings validate the verifier constraints configured in the task metadata (min_cross_sections=5, elevation bounds [900, 980]). All workspace output directories and file name targets exactly match the environment definitions.
